repo_prior_art.cite: none; the closest catalogue Stat entries concern dose-response minimax or weak-event ratios, not a planned illness-death initiation-hazard EIF.

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased derives an EIF for a closely related smoothed illness-death intervention; the externally fixed q_b intervention remains distinct.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference, bounded-count-diameter: rare-event causal-ratio inference, not a CAR illness-death marked-Poisson score experiment.

published_prior_art.cite: SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous gives effective-size theory for static tilted exposures, while AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment and ColangeloLee2023ContinuousDML give Gaussian continuous-treatment inference, not this finite-count multistate limit.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference, bounded-count-diameter: a close honest-diameter analogue for PN/PS ratios, but with a different causal target and no initiation-time intervention.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI supplies Hölder bias-aware CI geometry, but no learned CAR illness-death score or bounded-effective-count calibration.

soundness_flags.label: def:model-class, def:bias-aware-interval, and oeq:honest-diameter
soundness_flags.one_line: ass:score-integrability contains the free bandwidth b, but P_s is declared as a class of P alone; specify whether it holds for every b, for a stated sequence b_n, or define a bandwidth-indexed model class before defining honesty and D-star.

soundness_flags.label: lem:extension-invariance
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated hypotheses do not ensure local positive initiation density: conditional positivity alone does not preclude zero untreated eligibility survival without a local hazard-integrability/boundedness condition; include ass:bounded-hazards or an explicit positive risk-set condition.

soundness_flags.label: lem:uncensored-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed W_0=P,b=W_1=P,b=1 is false under the proposal's own zero-after-exhaustion conventions; restrict equality to active supports and state that the inactive integrands are zero.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:observed-hazard-validity, ass:car, and ass:compatible-extension
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The causal interpretation assumes, rather than identifies from observed data, that every compatible full-data extension obeys the displayed intervention-validity and censoring restrictions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The exact initiation-time causal target and extension-invariance claims are conditional on P_full belonging to mathfrak E(P); the critical-limit questions are observed-law statistical claims conditional on that causal setup.

source_verification_receipts.locator: Abstract; §3.1 and Appendix proof links in the primary arXiv version
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper studies an illness-death stochastic intervention modifying the no-treatment-to-initiation hazard, smooths a fixed-time target, derives an EIF, and proposes a debiased one-step estimator.
The paper studies exponentially tilted stochastic interventions for static continuous exposures, derives an EIF and minimax/convergence results with effective sample size n/delta.
The paper studies weighted local-linear GDRF estimation, uniform Bahadur representations, bootstrap confidence bands, and data-driven bias control.
The article develops honest bias-aware kernel/local-polynomial confidence intervals, including efficiency and bandwidth/length comparisons.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of Business and Economic Statistics 44(1) (2026), pp. 67–79
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives kernel-localized, cross-fitted doubly robust inference for continuous-treatment response functions and partial effects under nuisance-rate conditions.

source_verification_receipts.version: Preprint corresponding to the cited JASA article; journal theorem text not inspected
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The work develops cross-fitted doubly robust machine-learning inference for treatment-specific survival curves, pointwise and uniformly in time.
The article derives minimax CATE rates in a Hölder-smooth model and analyzes a local-polynomial estimator.

source_verification_receipts.version: JRSS B 79(4) (2017), pp. 1229–1245, version of record
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article develops kernel-smoothed doubly robust continuous-treatment effect estimation with asymptotic theory and bandwidth selection.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Make the statistical domain bandwidth-indexed, or impose an explicit uniform-in-b score and nuisance condition, so honesty and minimax diameter are defined over one unambiguous class.
Repair the active-support qualification in the uncensored reduction and add the risk-set survival condition needed for the Hölder version-selection lemma.
Turn the honest-diameter OEQ into a phase-diagram target with a named critical-law-calibrated interval and a concrete diameter order or lower-bound modulus.

recommended_next_step: Repair the three well-posedness/sanity defects, then retain this field-tier rare-initiation phase-transition angle for Stage 0.
